description: This X-ray diffraction (XRD) pattern displays the phase constitution of four Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.5C-based austenitic low-density steels: 3Cr, 2Ni, 2Ni3Cr, and 2Ni5Cr, which were homogenized at 1200°C for 2 h, hot-rolled with 95% reduction, and ultrafast-cooled at ~40°C/s. All four curves exhibit characteristic diffraction peaks of the face-centered cubic (FCC) γ-austenite phase, indexed as γ(111), γ(200), γ(220), γ(311), and γ(222), confirming that all alloys maintain a single-phase austenitic structure regardless of Ni and/or Cr addition. Notably, no diffraction peaks corresponding to the body-centered cubic (BCC) B2 phase are detected in any of the samples. The similar peak positions and relative intensities across all four compositions indicate that the synergistic addition of Ni and Cr does not alter the fundamental crystal structure of the matrix, supporting the conclusion that these alloying elements dissolve into the austenitic solid solution without forming secondary intermetallic phases detectable by XRD.
